heading: Rights-of-way for wagon roads or railroads
label: 525 Rights-of-way for wagon roads or railroads
text: In the form provided by existing law the Secretary of the Interior may file and approve surveys and plats of any right of way for a wagon road, railroad, or other highway over and across any national forest when in his judgment the public interests will not be injuriously affected thereby.
notes: CodificationAs originally enacted, this section contained following the word “forest” the words “or reservoir site”. See sections 665 and 958 of Title 43, Public Lands, which represent the phase of the section here omitted.“National forest” substituted in text for “forest reserve” on authority of , , which provided that forest reserves shall hereafter be known as national forests.act Mar. 4, 1907, ch. 290734 Stat. 1269

Repeal; Savings ProvisionSection repealed by , , , effective on and after , insofar as applicable to the issuance of rights-of-way over, upon, under, and through the public lands and lands in the National Forest System. Such repeal not to be construed as terminating any valid lease, permit, patent, etc., existing on , see , set out as a note under , Public Lands.Pub.
